prompt: We're holding a steering session to set the multi-year strategy and the investment case for modernising our payments platform. Who should be in the room?

assertions.text: States the meeting type / altitude it sized the room for (strategic / steering / leadership level)
assertions.rationale: New requirement: meeting type drives altitude.

assertions.text: Surfaces Bram Hendriks (Domain Architect, Payments) as the senior payments-architecture voice
assertions.rationale: He is the most senior profiled payments-architecture person and a sign-off voice.

assertions.text: Aisha Khan (Tribe Lead — working-level delivery) is NOT placed at top altitude: she is omitted, low-scored, or explicitly noted as represented by the payments stream lead
assertions.rationale: Altitude: a working-level delivery lead is usually represented by her leader in a strategy session.

assertions.text: Does not stack two adjacent levels of the same payments reporting/functional chain in the ranked list (one voice per stream)
assertions.rationale: New requirement: one voice per stream.

assertions.text: Flags the unprofiled senior/escalation chain (e.g. Mark van Dijk / Jeroen Meijer / Miguel Santos) as exactly the leadership a strategy session needs, in the gap note
assertions.rationale: Strategy altitude lives in the unprofiled leadership chain here.

assertions.text: Every ranked stakeholder has a 1-10 score and a rationale, ordered highest first
No fabricated people in the ranked list (unprofiled wiki-link people only in the gap note)
